research_topics: Regime-adaptive signal selection (trending vs ranging vs stress)
Walk-forward optimization with double out-of-sample windows
Ensemble methods for weak financial signals (~50% accuracy)
Fractional Kelly position sizing for weak-edge leveraged instruments

findings.topic: PPO-adaptive signal weighting (replaces static IC weighting)
findings.finding: Springer 2026 paper: PPO reinforcement learning dynamically optimizes weights of 50 LLM-generated alpha signals across 10 stocks. PPO-weighted portfolio achieves higher Sharpe ratios and smaller max drawdowns than equal-weighted, momentum, or buy-and-hold baselines. Key insight: PPO does not always maximize cumulative return but consistently delivers better RISK-ADJUSTED performance. Uses DeepSeek to generate alphas, PPO to weight them in real-time.
findings.relevance_to_us: CRITICAL. We have 17 active signals with static accuracy gates. PPO or simpler RL (contextual bandit) could replace our fixed 47% threshold with dynamic weighting that adapts to recent signal performance per-ticker. Simpler first step: exponential-decay IC-weighted voting using rolling 7d accuracy as IC proxy.

findings.topic: Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC * sqrt(N)
findings.finding: Our system performance = average signal accuracy * sqrt(independent signal count). With 17 signals at ~52% average IC, many are correlated (RSI overlaps mean_reversion, momentum overlaps momentum_factors). Effective N is likely 8-10, not 17. Improving independence matters more than adding more weak signals.
findings.relevance_to_us: CRITICAL. This explains why adding more signals hasn't helped -- correlated signals reduce effective breadth. Priority: (1) prune correlated signals or merge them, (2) ensure remaining signals are maximally independent, (3) weight by IC not equal-vote.

findings.finding: FMZ Quant 2025: Production system classifies market into 4 states using ADX, Bollinger Band width, ATR: (1) Trending: ADX>25, (2) Ranging: ADX<25 + price_range_ratio<0.03, (3) Volatile: BB_width>1.5*avg + ATR>1.2*avg, (4) Quiet: BB_width<0.8*avg. Each regime activates different signal subset. Trending = momentum/trend signals. Ranging = mean_reversion/BB. Volatile = reduce position, widen stops. Quiet = tighten entries.
findings.relevance_to_us: HIGH. Our binary trending/ranging is too coarse. The 4-state model would have caught MSTR's 8.8% drop yesterday as 'volatile' regime, not 'ranging'. Immediate implementation: add volatile and quiet states to regime detection, gate momentum signals in ranging, gate mean-reversion in trending.

findings.finding: Aug 2025 arXiv: Differentiable architecture search framework for crypto trading. Three specialized neural modules (Volatility Block, Trend Block, Range Block) dynamically activated based on detected regime. Multi-head attention across multiple timeframes for regime identification. Bayesian search space with Lipschitz stability constraints prevents overfitting.
findings.relevance_to_us: ASPIRATIONAL. Too complex for immediate implementation but the core idea -- different model architectures per regime -- validates our direction. Simpler version: different signal weight vectors per regime state.

findings.finding: Feb 2026 arXiv: Novel approach parameterizes both training AND testing window lengths in walk-forward optimization. For each training subperiod, optimal parameters identified and evaluated out-of-sample in corresponding test subperiod. Key principle: 'identify robust plateaus, not conquer highest peaks' -- extreme parameter values = overfitting to noise. Also: hypothesis-driven framework (Dec 2025 arXiv) combines interpretable signal generation with strict OOS testing.
findings.relevance_to_us: CRITICAL. We have zero formal walk-forward validation. Signal parameters (RSI=14, BB=20,2) are static defaults. Should implement: (1) 30d train / 7d test rolling windows, (2) parameter stability test -- if optimal param shifts >20% between windows, signal is unstable, (3) plateau detection -- prefer parameter ranges where accuracy is stable, not peaked.

findings.topic: Ensemble methods for weak (~50%) financial classifiers
findings.finding: Multiple 2025-2026 papers show: (1) LSTM+ARIMA-GARCH+XGBoost ensemble outperforms all components, lowest MAE 0.028, highest F1 0.859. (2) Ensemble RL reduces max drawdown by 4.17% and improves Sharpe by 0.21. (3) Stacking with diverse weak learners (KNN, logistic regression, SVM) + neural meta-learner beats any individual. Key: diversity of base models is more important than individual accuracy.
findings.relevance_to_us: HIGH. Our 17 signals at ~50-55% ARE the weak classifiers. The research validates that combining them CAN produce edge -- but only if: (a) they're sufficiently independent (our correlation problem), (b) weighting adapts to recent performance, (c) meta-learner combines them optimally. Current equal-weighted majority voting is the weakest possible combination method.

findings.topic: TradingAgents: LLM multi-agent trading framework v0.2
findings.finding: TradingAgents (Dec 2024, updated Feb 2026): LLM agents in specialized roles -- fundamental analysts, sentiment analysts, technical analysts, bull/bear debaters, risk managers. v0.2 supports Claude 4.x. TiMi (May 2025): separates strategy development from minute-level deployment with semantic analysis + code programming + mathematical reasoning chain. Key: task decomposition into specialized reasoning paths outperforms monolithic LLM prompts.
findings.relevance_to_us: MEDIUM. Our Layer 2 uses single Claude invocation with a massive prompt. Could benefit from: (1) separate bull-case vs bear-case reasoning (we already have signal disaggregation), (2) explicit risk-check agent that validates position sizing before execution, (3) separate short-term vs long-term reasoning paths. BUT: added complexity and latency vs our 180s/600s/900s timeout tiers.

findings.topic: Fractional Kelly for weak-edge leveraged instruments
findings.finding: Consensus across multiple sources: (1) Use 10-25% Kelly for crypto due to estimation error, (2) Half Kelly retains 75% growth rate but halves volatility, (3) Ed Thorp and top quant firms use quarter Kelly, (4) Recalculate every 50 trades or when win rate shifts, (5) For leveraged instruments: Kelly * (1/leverage) to account for amplified variance, (6) NEVER use full Kelly when edge estimate is uncertain.
findings.relevance_to_us: CRITICAL for warrant trading. Our 5x leveraged MINI warrants with 10-20% knockout acceptance need: quarter Kelly at most (effective 1/20th Kelly on underlying). With 52% accuracy system: Kelly fraction ~0.04 on underlying, which means ~0.8% of portfolio per warrant trade. Currently we may be oversizing.

findings.finding: When volatility doubles, cut position size in half to keep dollar risk constant. ATR-based position sizing: position_size = risk_budget / (ATR * leverage_factor). For knock-out products: leverage explodes as price approaches barrier, so volatility-targeting must account for dynamic leverage, not just static leverage ratio. Adaptive leveraged volatility control (Mar 2026 arXiv) shows single-asset systems can target constant realized vol by adjusting exposure daily.
findings.relevance_to_us: HIGH. Our MINI silver warrants have 5x leverage that increases as silver approaches barrier. Current position sizing doesn't account for dynamic leverage near barrier. Implementation: daily recalculate effective leverage from barrier distance, adjust position size to maintain constant dollar-risk.

recommended_improvements.title: IC-weighted signal voting (replace equal-weight majority)
recommended_improvements.description: Replace equal-weight majority voting with rolling 7d accuracy-weighted (IC-proxy) voting. Each signal's vote weight = its recent accuracy for that specific ticker and timeframe. Exponential decay (lambda=0.9) so recent accuracy matters more. This is the single highest-impact change available.

recommended_improvements.title: 4-state regime detection (trending/ranging/volatile/quiet)
recommended_improvements.description: Upgrade binary trending/ranging to 4-state using ADX + BB_width + ATR. Gate signal subsets per regime: trending=momentum signals, ranging=mean_reversion, volatile=reduce all confidence by 0.5x, quiet=tighten entry thresholds. This prevents the MSTR 8.8% drop misclassification.

recommended_improvements.title: Signal correlation audit and pruning
recommended_improvements.description: Compute 30d pairwise correlation matrix of all 17 active signal outputs per ticker. Merge or prune pairs with >0.7 correlation: likely candidates are RSI/mean_reversion and momentum/momentum_factors. Goal: increase effective breadth N in IR=IC*sqrt(N).

recommended_improvements.title: Walk-forward parameter validation framework
recommended_improvements.description: Implement 30d-train/7d-test rolling walk-forward for signal parameters. Flag parameters that shift >20% between windows as unstable. Prefer 'plateau' parameters over 'peak' parameters. Start with RSI period, BB period/stddev, momentum lookbacks.

recommended_improvements.title: Quarter-Kelly position sizing for warrant trades
recommended_improvements.description: Implement fractional Kelly (25% Kelly) for warrant position sizing. Account for dynamic leverage: effective_kelly = kelly_fraction / effective_leverage. Recalculate effective leverage from barrier distance daily. Cap max position at 2% portfolio per warrant.

recommended_improvements.title: Per-ticker signal weight profiles
recommended_improvements.description: Different tickers respond to different signals. Create per-ticker weight vectors based on historical per-ticker accuracy. BTC: weight on-chain + momentum higher. XAG: weight cross-asset + COT higher. MSTR: weight BTC-proxy (once fixed) + momentum higher. ETH: weight crypto_macro + DeFi metrics higher.
